Однако практическая отработка застопорилась. Модифицированный «Клятвенный молот» оказался слишком тяжёлым. Даже после того, как Прюн удлинила рукоять, опираясь на принцип рычага, и попыталась применить технику умелого приложения силы, поднять его всё равно было крайне трудно, не говоря уже о том, чтобы использовать его в бою.
«Как же персонажи в романах так легко поднимают оружие, которое в несколько раз больше их самих?»
Она тяжело вздохнула, и в её голове внезапно всплыли фразы для призыва силы из сказочных книг.
«Смешно, в такое только маленькие дети верят», - презрительно скривила она губы.
«Но, опять же... чем это отличается от того, чтобы загадать желание богам? Попробовать вроде не помешает».
«Очнись! Мы же не в игру играем!»
«Но вокруг же всё равно никого нет...»
После бурной внутренней борьбы Прюн, покраснев, стиснула зубы и, наконец, решилась.
«Клятвенный молот, заключающий в себе силу тьмы! Яви мне свою истинную мощь! Снимаю печать...» Как только слова слетели с её губ, она крепко сжала рукоять молота и зажмурилась. И произошло чудо: железный молот, который раньше был невероятно тяжёлым, вдруг стал лёгким как перышко в её руке!
Все признаки указывали на то, что желание сбылось.
«Ммм... неужели всё так просто?»
Не веря своим глазам, она протянула вторую руку, чувствуя, как элементальная энергия стремительно собирается и концентрируется на её ладони. По мере того как сияние усиливалось, Прюн, охваченная крайним волнением и ожиданием, инстинктивно зажмурилась и задержала дыхание. Вскоре тяжёлый Глаз Бога опустился на её ладонь, и она, переполненная радостью победы, осторожно приоткрыла глаза, готовая увидеть фиолетовое сияние Электро - символ холодной ярости и беспощадности...
Однако в её ладони лежал Глаз Бога Анемо, излучающий характерное бирюзовое сияние.
Прюн застыла в ошеломлении на целых десять секунд.
«...Что?!»
В голове Прюн молниеносно пронеслись знакомые ей заметки об элементе Электро.
Она не сдержалась и выкрикнула в небо: «Эй, там, наверху! Это какая-то ошибка! Я же готовилась к Электро!!!»
Но после краткого замешательства рациональный ум охотницы взял верх. Как ни крути, это был настоящий Глаз Бога, неоспоримое доказательство силы.
Научиться принимать неожиданное - тоже часть взросления. Прюн быстро взяла себя в руки и молча протёрла Глаз Бога. «Ничего страшного, придётся перечитать главы об Анемо заново». В конце концов, сильные никогда не жалуются на обстоятельства, и даже если это всего лишь лёгкий ветерок, она использует его, чтобы развеять ловушки, которые расставила ведьма.
Она пробормотала это вполголоса, и уголки её губ непроизвольно изогнулись в едва заметной улыбке, которую она сама даже не заметила.
description.en:
### Character Details

Just exactly when Prune's grudge against the "witch" began, and what it is all about, is a story that remains untold by the many "writs of retribution" that can be found on the noticeboards of Mondstadt.
Ask any Mondstadter about the time they met the so-called "Witch Hunter," and you will likely hear of one of three types of reactions.
Those in the first group would tell you that they offered a kind smile before asking if she wanted a glass of Apple Cider. Those of the second persuasion would merely nod vaguely at her words, then ask where her family was. Finally, there are those like Kaeya, the third kind, who would not only listen to her plans of revenge, but also offer their own suggestions, practicable or otherwise.
To Prune, none of these three kinds of treatment is satisfactory.
Nevertheless, she had long since grown used to these responses. She has come to see that it is the fate of the hunter to be utterly misunderstood — and being treated like a child playing house is something she has to face every day.
The fact is, Prune does stand head and shoulders above her peers, and even some adults, in the sheer breadth of her knowledge. Her speech is clear, careful, and organized, and sometimes punctuated by a random factoid thrown in for good measure. The only real indication that she is still just a child is the look of quiet pride that unfailingly accompanies those little-known facts.
But once you get to know her better, you realize that the breadth of her knowledge and understanding is not exactly matched by a corresponding depth — after all, how much of the profound and important can a child really know? Consequently, the "facts" in her arsenal that she is so proud of are often the very ones that lead to her undoing during a "debate." And every time this happens, she bites her lower lip and retreats expeditiously, though not before muttering, "I'll be back."
But it would be a mistake to dismiss her as simply a little girl trying to act tough because of this.
When she says she will be back, return she will. In situations like this, she invariably shows up again the following day, armed with even more notes, more arguments, and an even more mulish look on her face.
Is this stubbornness of hers the grit required of a true hunter or simply childish petulance? This is a question that perhaps even she is unable to answer. One thing, however, she knows for certain: she will settle her score with that "wicked witch" once and for all, no matter how many tries it takes.

### Character Story 1

Prune's parents were Mondstadt natives who, seduced by pithy aphorisms like "Life is an open road," ended up traveling the world. They eventually settled in Nod-Krai, a land of chaos and opportunity. This land, teeming with merchants, adventurers, cheats, smugglers, etc., etc., was clearly no place to start a family and bring up a child. Her dad toiled at the docks while her mom worked two jobs at once. Money was tight, and they only made enough to keep Prune fed and warm. Anything else, like spending time with her, was a luxury they could not afford.
Once, on one of those rare occasions that her mother could make it home before dinner, Prune rushed out to meet her, tugging at her clothes in excitement to tell her about a toy seller in town who was selling these really pretty pinwheels from Mondstadt.
But as she held her mother's hands, made rough by hard work, she recalled the ledger hidden in her father's drawer, a book of numbers and letters detailing all they owed, and decided to bite her tongue instead.
"Those things do nothing but spin," she told herself. "Not exactly the most useful things to have around. Besides, I don't even like them. No, I don't like them at all."
She would tell herself things like this all the time.
Whenever she walked by a candy seller, she would tell herself softly, "I stopped eating these long ago. Sugar is bad for the teeth anyway."
And whenever the kids next door showed off a shiny new toy, she would sniff at them, proclaiming their behavior "totally childish."
Over time, without realizing it, young Prune had built a persona of maturity and poise around herself, a high wall of self-protection that would allow no childish longing to slip through.
She learned to heat her own lunches, mend her clothes, and even how to check the household accounts. Once, she absolutely beamed when she discovered that her mother had been shortchanged three days' pay, as it meant she was actually contributing in a "useful" way.
In the evenings, by the time her parents dragged their weary bodies home, Prune was already all washed up and lying in bed. Though she wasn't actually asleep, she knew her father and mother would be heartbroken to learn she was still afraid of the dark and needed them by her side, and she did not want to weigh down their existing burdens with an added layer of guilt.
"I'm no longer a kid," she would tell herself, and this became not just her pet phrase but her very own suit of armor.

### Character Story 2

Fortunately, there are still several pastimes in this world that do not cost very much, and reading is one of them.
Prune visited Mimisbrunnr Books every day to browse the shelves in silence. The owner didn't mind her being there, for he was far too busy pushing investment coupons on his wealthier customers to care about a little girl. So, left to her own devices, she made those myriad pages her very first window into the world.
She had no real system for picking what to read next; all that mattered was whether the cover or the title caught her eye. She started with the fairy tales with the colorful book jackets, but soon grew tired of the easy tropes and predictable happily-ever-afters. From there, she began to devour anything with text she could lay her hands on. But Nod-Krai wasn't like the libraries of Mondstadt or Sumeru. Instead of grand, curated collections, she found mostly yellowed newspapers or papers with "news" of things that never happened, terribly misleading travel guides, and pirated, poorly translated novels... and plodding historical tomes containing details no child her age was meant to see.
As her reading habit spiraled, Prune gradually became a hoarder of obscure knowledge, a fact she delightedly took as proof of her intellectual superiority. This haphazard collection of out-there facts in her young mind began to frame her understanding of the world in a manner that was complex, yet rather fuzzy, at the same time.
Did she really understand what she was reading? Most of the time, the answer was not really. But she was more than happy to soak up those difficult terms and alien concepts, like someone picking up every shiny seashell in sight on a beach and putting them all into a bag.
Even though she was invited to play with the neighborhood kids a few times, on those occasions, she either failed to hear them because she was lost in her own thoughts, or she spent the time obsessively studying the rules of the game using the vocabulary she had picked up from her books.
"Hold on!" Prune would interject as she raised her hand, "I must check the underlying logic of the game first."
"The Pirates had a real topographical advantage and a sufficient time buffer! They shouldn't have been defeated so quickly, unless the Conch Gang used an undefined Surprise Attack that caused the power-scaling system to fail!"
Slowly, over time, the other kids stopped knocking at her door.
Prune wasn't blind to the other kids drifting away, though she gave a little scoff to show she no longer cared. Besides, there were so many deep questions, complicated principles, and unexplained phenomena in the world waiting for her to apply her intellect and energy to.
She was already on a different path from the others.

### Character Story 3

It was an afternoon filled with restlessness and tangled thoughts.
Through sheer perseverance, Prune finally managed to finish the first volume of the "Hymns of the Far North" even though an adult had warned her it might be unsuitable for a child her age. Yet, by the time she snapped the book shut, she could hardly remember what she had actually read. All that remained was the sheer weight of those verses, a sense of mental exhaustion, and the loneliness of having no one to talk to about any of it.
And so, in her usual fashion, she began to expound to the empty air about what she had just read. In her mind's eye, she saw a little puppy listening, a creature that material circumstances made impossible for one to actually adopt. Or maybe she was speaking to one of the sages from the book who never said a word in return. She wasn't quite sure whom she was talking to, only that she needed to talk. But this time, the air began to stir, and the friend so alive in her imagination began to take shape before her.
"It's you... You are..."
Oh, how Prune wished to call it by its name! But it was at this juncture that she realized that she had been addressing it by "you" all this time.
It was imperative that the creature be given a name of its own then and there. The common names like "Floofy," "Barky," and "Tiny" were out, since it was clear to her that this was no ordinary fawning puppy. It had a keen intelligence visible in its slightly raised brows and a peculiar depth in the dark pools of its eyes. Its indigo and violet-dappled coat had a sense of mystery and steadiness that Prune found deeply pleasing.
"Descartes," she finally breathed. It was a perfect name, full of the spirit of intellectual inquiry and curiosity, and entirely worthy of a companion of hers. It was a name that also spoke to her own formidable erudition.
Descartes' arrival added quite the burst of color to Prune's daily life, even as it turned her into even more of a... lone ranger.
One afternoon, after the rains had come and gone. Prune was crouching by the street, studying a puddle intently.
"Look, Descartes. Look at how the world is upside down in the puddle. That raises the question: how do we know that we aren't just living in a bigger puddle?"
Several passersby slowed down as they passed, staring at the girl who appeared to be talking to herself.
"Maybe," Prune continued, "what we know as 'real' is just another 'reflection' in a higher dimension. Don't you agree, Descartes?"
She turned towards the air around her and nodded gravely.
"Yes, that's what I think too. So fundamentally, all knowledge is relative, and absolute truth does not exist."
Finally, a kind old lady walked over to her and asked with a touch of concern, "Hey, little girl. Are you... alright? Do you need me to call your family?"
Prune looked up and replied earnestly, "Hey granny. Do you believe that the world is real?"
"..."
"Never mind. That's not a question everyone asks even once in their lifetime anyway," Prune concluded. "Let's go, Descartes. Maybe living in an illusion is just what most adults do."
As she watched the odd little girl leave, the old lady made a sign over her chest and murmured, "May the moon watch over you, my child..."
However, there are corners in Nasha Town's narrow and winding streets and alleys where the light of the moon's blessing simply cannot reach.
And in those cold, solitary corners, the only "blessing" Prune had was Descartes, her silent but ever-faithful companion.

### Character Story 4

The bored witch, who had been looking for some kind of distraction, finally found a target in the girl buried deep in a giant book by the side of the road.
"Why aren't you playing with those kids over there?"
"I don't like to play." Prune, who was busy reading, didn't even look up.
"What are we looking at?" Alice moved her head to get a better look, and her eyes lit up as if she'd just discovered some kind of treasure.
"A travel guide of some sort. But it's missing some parts because it's a pirated copy. I'm not sure who the writer is..."
"How do you find it?"
"It's okay, I guess. But I can't help feeling that the writer is just incredibly self-absorbed. I also caught some troubling tendencies between the lines."
"Oh?"
From that day on, Alice would "run into" Prune every now and then, always hitting her with the most random of questions like "Does adding sugar to a Hydro Slime make it a sweet drink?" or "If you conquer yourself, do you actually win or lose?"
And each time Alice dropped one of these nonsensical provocations, Prune would rise to the challenge as if the enemy were literally at the gates, and scramble to dismantle these "false propositions" with her impeccable logic in a way that was both clumsy and adorable. Meanwhile, Alice would watch her exertions with chin in hand and a smile on her face, like she was taking in some sort of performance.
Through all of this, the astute Alice also noticed Prune's imaginary friend slowly fading, like a drop of water evaporating in the sunlight.
At first, Prune did not really care for the fact that Alice was a mage. After all, in Nod-Krai, it is not unusual to encounter individuals claiming to be one extraordinary character or another. Even the average guy queuing up for a Feast of the Moon might claim he's a retired master thief. A strange, flamboyant woman in an oversized hat who engaged in mischievous banter? She would totally fit in with the rest of the local personalities.
Little did she know that one day, this cheeky woman would turn into her nemesis...
"Did you just use a non-arithmetic method to prove the relationship between the sides of a triangle? Impressive."
"Excuse me, but that's a problem even a 12-year-old can solve."
"Well, how about we try another problem for 14-year-olds then? Come on, let's see who can solve it first!"
"Don't you have anything better to do than hang out with someone my age? I have to go home for dinner. Let's go, Descartes."
But unfortunately, Descartes had almost faded into nothingness by this point.
Seeing this, Alice immediately threw herself into the role of a stage villain. "Heh heh heh..." she cackled. "I am actually a wicked, wicked witch! And now, I'm snatching away your most precious friend!"
"W—What!?"
"I'm talking about... HIM!" Then, Alice snapped her fingers, and Descartes vanished from Prune's sight.
Quick-witted as Prune usually is, she experienced a rare system crash at this moment.
"What is wrong with you!?" she cried, clutching desperately at Alice. "What did you do to Descartes??? I want my Descartes back!!!"
"Oops, too late~ From now on, you're officially the 'Witch Hunter' appointed by the Great Mage herself! Want to save your imaginary friend? You'll have to beat me first!"
"You call yourself a mage??? That's straight-up witch behavior! Give me back my Descartes now!!!"
"Ha! Just kidding!" Alice was really hitting her stride with that wicked laugh now. "Want your imaginary friend back? Then you'll have to best every mage on the planet! That's right, you'll have to beat them all up, one by one!"
"Go to Mondstadt and find Klee. Who knows, you might just get a clue or two from the world's most adorable little kid."
Then, Alice disappeared with a bang and a poof.
"ALICE!!! YOU BIG, FAT, BADDIE!!!"
And so, poor Prune's long, arduous campaign to save her friend began.

### Character Story 5

Prune began her "witch-hunting" campaign as soon as she landed in Mondstadt. She put up notices of a rather antagonistic nature everywhere, tried to publicly confront the astrologer Mona (suspected to be the witch known as "B"), and sought out various individuals linked to her targets... But as the setbacks accumulated, she had no choice but to consider even more robust measures.
A rather outrageous idea came to her mind: she'd abduct Klee to force Alice to show herself.
The plan was simple. She would lure her target to somewhere quiet, then perform a thorough interrogation to obtain the intel she needed. Prune even went so far as to map out the operation in her notebook.
But of course, as a "Witch Hunter" with real scruples, she then very quickly ditched the idea.
She came to this decision not because she would also have to contend with Albedo and all of the Knights of Favonius (although it would admittedly be the greatest obstacle), but because in the tug-of-war between reason and impulse, morality eventually triumphed over hate.
"'He who slays the monster invariably becomes one himself,' right? Please, that's such an unoriginal tactic! I would never stoop to doing something so despicable to a child!"
She drew a giant cross over her "operational plan," flipped to a fresh page, and wrote down two words, "Plan B," before staring blankly at her notebook.
The days passed as Prune plotted her next steps, often to the sounds of Klee walking in on her with snacks or a toy in hand.
"Prune, the sun is out today! Let's go catch some Crystalflies!"
"Prune, it's raining today. Let's go jump some puddles with Mini Durin!"
"Prune~~~"
Then, at some point, Prune realized that she had unwittingly become Klee's playmate, and in this role, she was "forced" to doodle on walls with Klee, listen to her introduce the name and personality of every single Dodoco in complete seriousness, and even roll down the grassy slopes of Windrise...
"...I'm just collecting intel for my operation," Prune would say to herself each time.
Finally, after several of these "intelligence-gathering outings," Prune hammered out a truly viable strategy. She was going to obtain the intel she needed by grilling Klee, not by outright interrogation but by disguising the questioning as a game.
The operation would be a simple and straightforward one. Prune would ask the questions, and reward Klee with a freshly baked morsel each time her target provides an answer. She would start with a broad, innocent question, then probe deeper and deeper from there, gradually closing in on the key intel while her target's guard was completely down.
"Klee, have you received any letters from your mom recently?"
"Yeah, yeah. I get letters from her pretty often, and we also talk on the Dodocomm when there aren't any letters. I'd like a chocolate cookie, please."
"So, Klee, are you aware of your mom's atrocities... um... I mean, actions?"
"A toast with cheese...? I don't remember my mom ever making that. That sounds yummy though! It'll be so good with a glass of milk!"
"Is your mom in some kind of strongh— uh, I mean, does she have some kind of secret base?"
"A secret base! I know where it is!"
"Where is it???"
"It's... in the heart! Mom says that the safest secret base for everyone is in their hearts!"
Prune's rewards from an afternoon of "grilling" were seven drawings by Klee, including five on "How to Use a Jumpy Dumpty" and one depicting a Dodoco family. The last, drawn at Klee's insistence, was titled "Prune and Klee Blow Up Fish Together." Additionally, Prune also learned — quite by accident — the exact safe distance to set off a Jumpy Dumpty, though it was also at this point she realized there was serious work to be done if one ever wanted to bring Klee up to speed on the actual definition of the word "safe."
Early evening. Albedo had come to pick up Klee, asking her tenderly if she had a good day.
Klee let out a bright, cheery smile as she patted her bursting-full tummy in total satisfaction.
"Prune's cookies are so yummy! I won't need dinner tonight!"
Meanwhile, Prune was writing up her "After-Action Report: Operation Bake-and-Grill, Round 1" with utter seriousness.
"Usable intel: None. Supplies consumed: 1 bag of flour, 5 bottles of milk, and a substantial amount of chocolate and sugar. NB: Target appears to have an innate talent for counter-interrogation, and there are currently no known methods that can be used to break her."
She paused for a moment before adding another line below in tiny letters. It read:
"That picture of us blowing up fish together isn't too bad, actually. Filed in dossier."

### "Witchlure Bell" and "Hexhunter Oathhammer"

Descartes's "abduction" had plunged Prune into a kind of worry she had never known before.
Like a warrior preparing for imminent action, she shut herself away in her room to review every detail of her confrontations with Alice. Whenever the two of them reached a point where Alice could no longer keep up the debate, the Hexenzirkel mage would suddenly produce a "collector's item" with a beaming smile to reward Prune for her victory.
At first, Prune thought nothing of these "collector's items." Surely they were nothing more than cheap bribes, like the candies adults stuff your face with to stop you from talking.
Then, a piercing doubt struck her: given how powerful mages can be, could a "trifle" from one of them really be nothing more than a toy?
She reached for all the prizes she had won from Alice and laid them out for a thorough assessment. But first, she had to eliminate the obvious distractions: assorted snacks in fancy packaging, stickers with juvenile designs, plush toys with no special functions... Finally, she zeroed in on the two items with the most potential.
The first was a bell of exquisite workmanship. "When the bell rings, a witch might appear," Alice had said rather ambiguously. From this, Prune deduced it was a witch-catcher of some sort. In truth, it was an old tea party bell; the "might" in Alice's claim stemmed from the fact that attendance was never mandatory, leaving it a mystery who — if anyone — would show up. But now that Prune has redefined its purpose, the bell has indeed played a vital role in her adventures. While ringing it never guarantees a witch, it also never fails to bring on some really incredible adventures.
The second item was a heavy, and visually striking, hammer. It was the largest and heaviest of all the prizes Prune had won from Alice, appearing truly formidable and intimidating whenever it was swung. Little did she know that this was actually a sculpting tool Alice had originally planned to give to another mage, Nicole, as a present, though this rather over-the-top gift was subsequently (and politely) rejected by the intended recipient, who said, "My hobby is sculpting, not construction and demolition." As a result, it had been sitting around collecting dust until Alice finally found the perfect excuse to offload it.
These two gifts from the "Dodo-King" were then ceremoniously renamed after some very logical and practical weapon modifications on Prune's part...
... And now, they are known as the "Witchlure Bell" and the "Hexhunter Oathhammer."

### Vision

Prune had once heard it said that "As long as you wish hard enough, the gods will surely cast their eyes upon you." Assuming this was true, all she had to do to obtain the Electro Vision she so very much wanted was to make that wish with all her might.
Prune had plenty of reasons to choose Electro: Pyro was far too fervent, Hydro was too mellow, Geo felt unwieldy, Cryo was a bit haughty, Anemo could be rather capricious, and Dendro leaned toward vivaciousness. Of all the elements, only Electro was cold and swift — the perfect choice to strike down that cunning witch with.
One only has to pause for a moment to realize that none of these "reasons" can really stand up to scrutiny. Maybe, just maybe, the one factor that truly tipped the scales in Electro's favor was the fact that the purple of the Electro Vision would be a perfect match for her carefully curated black-and-heavy-metal wardrobe...
Her goal identified, this extraordinary young scholar-turned-hunter then set out to attain it with a means just as extraordinary: pre-study.
She sat down to read the "Guide to Elemental Power" cover to cover not once, but three times. She committed every detail regarding Electro to memory, and even mentally simulated various "Hunter power combos" with the element in mind.
But she soon found herself stymied when the time came to put her ideas into practice. Her very logical and practical modifications to the Hexhunter Oathhammer had made it far too heavy to hold, let alone fight with, even after she modded it further with a longer handle based on the lever principle and attempted to apply a more technical touch to her wielding.
"How in the world did those characters in the books actually manage to lift weapons several times their size...?"
She let out a heavy sigh. Then, suddenly, she recalled the words those fairytale characters used whenever they summoned their powers.
"Oh come on. Only little kids would believe in something like this," she told herself, her lips pulling into a pout.
"But then again... What do I have to lose? How is that any different from making a wish to a god?"
"I've got to snap out of this. This isn't some game of play-pretend..."
"But since no one's around anyway..."
After much internal wrangling, Prune finally steeled herself, and took the leap with flushed cheeks and gritted teeth —
"O Hexhunter Oathhammer, you that contain the powers of darkness, reveal your true strength before me! UNLEASH!!!" The words had barely left her lips, her eyes still squeezed shut as she gripped the handle tight. But something astonishing had already happened. That ponderous, unwieldy hammer had become as light as a feather in an instant.
The evidence was undeniable. All the signs pointed to her wish coming true.
"Is... is that it!? Is that all I had to do???"
She stretched out her other hand incredulously. As the elemental energy coalesced in her palm, a light began to glow; so overcome with anticipation was Prune that she closed her eyes and even stopped breathing for a second. A brief moment later, she felt the weight of a Vision fall into her hand. With pure joy, she slowly opened her eyes to take in that purple radiance signifying cold-blooded savagery...
The only thing was — the object sitting in her palm was an Anemo Vision with its characteristic green glow.
It was several moments before Prune could respond to this unexpected development.
"What the—"
Images of the voluminous notes on Electro she had pored over and memorized started flashing through her mind.
She looked up at the skies, her indignation clear. "Hey, whoever's up there: There must be some kind of mistake! I was studying for Electro!!!"
But the rational hunter returned once the initial shock passed. No matter how it looked, this was a Vision, and irrefutable proof of one's strength, alright.
Learning to deal with the unexpected is part and parcel of growing up. Prune gathered herself and quietly gave the Vision a good wipe. "Never mind. I'll just have to study the Anemo chapters again." After all, winners are winners because they make the best of what they've got, and if she's been handed a gust of wind, she'll use that very wind to scatter whatever traps the witch has laid for her.
As she continued to mutter to herself, an almost imperceptible smile appeared on her face.
description.zh:
### 角色详细

布伦妮与魔女之间到底有着怎样苦大仇深的过节，在蒙德许多告示板上的「讨伐檄文」留有些许只言片语的记载。
关于「魔女猎人」的故事，蒙德市民们的反应大致可以分为三类。
第一类是善意的微笑，然后问她要不要来一杯苹果酿。第二类是似懂非懂地点点头，然后问她家里的大人在哪里。第三类是以凯亚为代表的行动派，不但认真聆听她的复仇计划，还会予以具体的建议，无论是否具备可操作性。
布伦妮对以上三种反应都不太满意。
但不满意归不满意，她也已经习惯了。在她看来，不被理解是猎人的宿命，而被当成玩过家家的小孩，更是她每天都要面对的目光。
事实上，单论见识的广度，布伦妮确实领先于许多同龄人乃至部分成年人。她说话条理清晰、措辞严谨，偶尔还会不经意地抛出令人意想不到的冷知识，并因此露出一个藏不住的得意小表情——那是她少数不加掩饰的孩子气瞬间。
但相处久了就会发现，她的知识版图虽然辽阔却并不深厚。毕竟，一个小孩能知道什么真正重要的大事呢？所以她引以为傲的见识，往往在关键时刻被现实反将一军。每当这种时候，她会咬着嘴唇留下一句「我一定会回来的」，头也不回地撤退。
不过，如果有人因此觉得她只是个逞强的小鬼头，那恐怕要失算了。
因为她说「会回来」，就真的会回来。第二天，她一定带着更充分的论据、更厚的笔记和一张更加不服气的脸重新站到你面前。
这种不肯认输的执拗，到底是猎人的天赋还是小孩子的赌气，也许连她自己都分不清楚。但至少有一点可以确定——不管需要折腾多少次，她与那位邪恶魔女之间的账，一笔都不会少算。

### 角色故事1

布伦妮的父母是蒙德人，年轻时，他们被「人生是旷野」这类浪漫宣言打动，背着行囊环游世界，最终在挪德卡莱这个充满机遇与混乱的地方定居下来。这里鱼龙混杂，商人、冒险家、骗子、走私贩交织成一张复杂的人际网络，显然不是什么适合养育孩子的地方。爸爸在码头找了个辛苦的差事，妈妈同时打两份工，一家人日子过得紧巴巴。他们对布伦妮的爱只能尽量让她吃饱穿暖，至于陪伴，那就像浪子的真心一样奢侈。
有一次，妈妈难得在晚饭前回家，布伦妮兴奋地跑到门外迎接，攥着妈妈的衣角，迫不及待地想告诉她，镇上有个商人在卖蒙德的风车玩具，特别漂亮。
但当她牵着妈妈粗糙的双手，又想起爸爸藏在抽屉那本密密麻麻记录着欠款的账簿，只好把话咽回肚子里。
「那种只会转圈圈的东西，一点都不实用。」她在心里对自己说「而且我也不喜欢，真的不喜欢。」
这样的话，她已经对自己说过许多次了。
路过糖果摊时，她会小声安慰自己「甜食对牙齿不好，我早就不吃这些了。」
看到隔壁小孩抱着新玩具炫耀，她会扬起下巴说「太幼稚了吧。」
日复一日的自我说服，让小小的布伦妮在不知不觉中，给自己竖起了一道名为「成熟懂事」的高墙。
她学会了自己热饭菜，自己缝补衣服，甚至能帮忙核对账簿上的数字，有一次她还发现雇主少算了妈妈三天的工钱，能帮上这些「有用」的忙，可把自己得意坏了。
每天夜里，当爸爸妈妈拖着疲惫的身躯回到家时，布伦妮早就已经洗漱完毕躺在床上，假装睡着了。因为她知道，如果让他们发现自己还醒着、还怕黑、还需要陪伴，他们会自责，会难过，会在本就沉重的负担上再重重压上一层愧疚。
「我已经不是小孩了。」这句话成了她的口头禅，也成了她的盔甲。

### 角色故事2

好在，世界上还有不少不怎么花钱的爱好，读书就是其中比较划算的一项。
「秘谜书屋」的店主似乎只专注向有购买力的目标客户兜售投资券，并不在意每天前来蹲在书架前安静翻书的布伦妮，于是各种崭新或泛黄的书页成了她最初认知世界的窗口。
她阅读的范围毫无体系，全凭封面和书名是否吸引。起初是色彩斑斓的童话绘本，但她很快便不满足于王子公主的幸福结局。她开始啃噬一切能接触到的、带字的东西。但不像蒙德和须弥的图书馆，挪德卡莱充斥着更多的是：过期或铺满假新闻的报纸、误导性的旅行指南、错版盗版或者翻译有误的小说、若干少儿不宜的沉重历史读物。
随着阅读量的激增，布伦妮渐渐成为了一名掌握诸多冷门知识的「情报强者」，并对这种智力上的优越感产生了一种洋洋自得的愉悦。这些零碎而超前的知识，过早地在她脑海中拼凑出一个复杂且略带灰色的认知框架。
她读得懂吗？大多数时候，只是一知半解，甚至完全不懂。但她乐此不疲地把那些艰深的术语、陌生的概念像收集闪亮的贝壳一样装进脑子里。
邻里孩子们玩耍时邀请过她几次，但她要么沉浸在自己的思绪中没听见，要么加入后，下意识地用她学来的词汇去分析游戏规则。
「等一下！」布伦妮举起手，「我需要确认一下游戏的底层逻辑。」
「『海盗』拥有足够的地形优势和时间缓冲窗口，不应该这么快被攻破，除非『海螺帮』使用了未被明确定义的『奇袭』，导致战力系统失衡！」
玩伴们面面相觑，渐渐便不再来找她。
布伦妮并非没有察觉这种疏离，但她冷哼一声，表示已经无所谓了。世界上有那么多深奥的谜题、复杂的原理、等待被理解的现象，那些才是真正值得投入时间的东西。
她和他们，已经走在不同的路上了。

### 角色故事3

那是一个思绪纷扰的下午。
尽管有大人告诫过「小孩子不要看这些书」，但布伦妮依旧艰难地啃完了《终北祷歌集》的第一卷。合上书，前面忘了、后面忘了、中间也记不太清了，但那些晦涩的诗句，对现在的布伦妮而言还是太过沉重，智力探索的疲惫与无人分享的孤独感，如潮水般弥漫开来。
于是，她像往常一样，对着身旁的空气开启了单方面的长篇大论。在她的想象中，一直有一个倾听者——或许是那只受限于现实条件没法收养的流浪小狗，或许是书中某个深沉寡言的智者。她不知道自己究竟在对「谁」说话，只是日复一日地将无处安放的分享欲倾注其中。可是这一次，空气轻轻晃动，幻想中的朋友清晰地浮现在眼前。
「是你吧…你就是…」
布伦妮激动地想要呼喊它的名字，却才发现，自己在脑海中一直以「你」来称呼它。
当务之急是取一个名字。首先要排除「毛球」、「大嗓门」或「小不点」之类的宠物名。显然，它不是那种爱撒娇的寻常小狗：微微皱起的眉头彰显别样的睿智，眼眸深邃叫人看不透它的内心，最令人叫绝的是它那蓝紫交杂的毛色，如此神秘又沉稳，非常符合布伦妮当前的审美。
「德斯卡戴」，她选择了这个充满哲学思辨气息的名字，既确立了伙伴的格调，又顺便展现了自己卓越的学识。
德斯卡戴的到来，不仅极大地丰富了布伦妮的生活，也让她显得更…特立独行。
一个雨后的下午，布伦妮蹲在街边观察水坑里的倒影。
「你看，德斯卡戴，这个水坑里的世界和真实的世界是完全相反的。那么问题来了，我们怎么证明自己不是活在某个『更大的水坑』里呢？」
路过的行人放慢脚步，用奇怪的眼神看着这个对着水坑自言自语的小女孩。
「也许，」布伦妮继续说，「我们所认为的『真实』，只是某种更高维度存在的『倒影』。你说对不对，德斯卡戴？」
她转头看向身边的空气，严肃地点了点头。
「嗯，我也这么想。所以本质上，所有的认知都是相对的，绝对真理根本不存在。」
一位好心的老婆婆走过来，关切地问：「小妹妹，你…没事吧？要不要帮你找找家人？」
布伦妮抬起头，眼神真诚地问：「这位婆婆，您相信这个世界是真实的吗？」
「…」
「算了，很多人到老都没思考过这个问题。」布伦妮站起来，「走吧，德斯卡戴，也许活在虚影中就是许多大人的日常。」
老婆婆目送着这个古怪的小女孩离开，忍不住在胸口比了个祈祷的手势：「愿月光庇佑你，孩子…」
然而，那夏镇的街巷逼仄错落，悲悯的月光终有照不透的幽暗角落。
在这冰冷的现实缝隙里，真正如影随形「庇佑」着布伦妮的，其实只有德斯卡戴——它无声却恒久的陪伴，填满了小女孩所有的独处时光。

### 角色故事4

百无聊赖的魔女为了打发时间盯上了路边正在抱着一本大书研读的小女孩。
「怎么不去跟那边的小孩玩？」
「不喜欢『玩』。」布伦妮甚至没有抬起头，继续翻书。
「在看什么呢？」艾莉丝探头一看，眼睛明显亮了起来，像是发现了什么宝藏。
「游览指南之类的东西。但因为是盗版，只印了些段落，作者不详…」
「你觉得写得怎么样？」
「还行吧。感觉作者是那种极度以自我为中心的人，字里行间还透露出不少恶趣味。」
「哦呀？」
从那天起，艾莉丝隔三岔五就会「碰巧」出现在布伦妮身边，抛出五花八门的话题，发起「辩论」：「往水史莱姆里加糖会不会得到一杯甜味饮料？」「战胜自我是我赢了还是我输了？」…
艾莉丝沉浸在这种无厘头的挑衅上，而布伦妮则每次都如临大敌，她会试图用严谨的逻辑链条去驳斥这些「伪命题」，过于认真的模样透露着笨拙的可爱。艾莉丝则托着腮，笑眯眯地看着她引经据典，仿佛在欣赏一场有趣的演出。
与此同时，艾莉丝敏锐地注意到，布伦妮身边那位幻想朋友的身影正变得越来越淡，如同阳光下即将蒸发的晨露。
起初，布伦妮对于艾莉丝的「魔女」身份不以为意。在挪德卡莱，连一起排队领「飨月餐」的普通大叔，都可能自称是隐退的神奇大盗。那么，街头多出一个头戴镂空大帽子、审美浮夸且言辞幼稚的奇怪阿姨，也完全符合这里的人类多样性。
她没想到，有一天，这个大龄幼稚鬼竟会成为她命定般的宿敌——
「用数学以外的方法证明三角形边长的关系，这种难题已经难不倒你了吗？了不起。」
「阿姨，这个最多属于十二岁水平的基础题。」
「那么，我再出一个十四岁级别的题，来比比看吧！」
「你是只能在这个年龄段混了吗？我要回家吃饭了。德斯卡戴，我们走。」
可惜这时的德斯卡戴已经几近透明，微弱得快要消失了。
「哼哼。」艾莉丝突然摆出一副极其夸张的反派表情，「我其实是邪恶的大魔女！现在…我要把你最重要的朋友抓走！」
「什…什么？」
「就是『它』啦——」艾莉丝打了个清脆的响指，德斯卡戴的身影在布伦妮眼中彻底消散了。
布伦妮聪明的小脑瓜罕见地宕机了。
「你有病吧！」她死死抓住艾莉丝的衣角，「你对德斯卡戴做了什么！快还给我！」
「来不及了哦~从现在开始，你就是被大魔女亲自认证的『魔女猎人』了！想要救回你的幻想朋友，就必须先打败我！」
「魔女和巫婆是有本质区别的好吧！这根本是巫婆行为！快把德斯卡戴还给我！」
「嗯哼，骗你的。」艾莉丝笑得更加肆意了，「想要救回幻想朋友，要打败世界上所有的魔女才可以哦！对，她们也要挨揍。」
「到蒙德去吧，找到可莉，那位全天下最可爱的小朋友说不定会给你线索哦。」
说完，艾莉丝便消失在爆炸后的烟雾中。
「艾——莉——丝！你这个无耻的大反派！」
就这样，布伦妮漫长的讨伐之旅，在气急败坏的咆哮中正式拉开帷幕。

### 角色故事5

刚到蒙德，布伦妮便立刻展开了她的猎魔行动。张贴火药味十足的告示、试图与疑似「魔女B」的占星术士莫娜当街对质、走访调查魔女的关系网…但随着各项行动屡屡受挫，她不得不开始考虑一些更极端的手段。
一个疯狂的念头在脑海闪过——绑架可莉，逼艾莉丝现身。
计划很简单：将目标引至人少的地方，进行残酷的拷问，获取魔女的核心情报。布伦妮甚至已经在笔记本上画好了行动路线图。
当然了，作为一个有底线的魔女猎人，她很快就否决了这个方案。
不是因为可莉身后有阿贝多和整个西风骑士团做靠山（当然这无疑是最大的实战阻碍），而是在理智与冲动的拉锯中，道德最终战胜了仇恨。
「『屠龙者终成恶龙』么…老套！欺负小孩算什么嘛，我才不干这种无耻的事。」
她在行动路线图上画上一个大大的叉，翻到新的一页，写下了「方案B」三个字，盯着空白的纸面开始发呆。
在布伦妮盘算下一步策略的那些日子里，可莉数不清多少次拿着零食和玩具推门而入。
「布伦妮姐姐，今天出太阳了，我们去抓晶蝶吧！」
「布伦妮姐姐，今天下雨啦，我们去找小杜林哥哥一起踩水坑！」
「布伦妮姐姐——」
一来二去，布伦妮发现自己不知不觉变成了可莉的玩伴。她「被迫」陪可莉在墙面上涂鸦、听可莉一本正经地介绍每一只嘟嘟可的名字和性格、甚至被拉着在风起地的草坡上打滚…
「…我是在做情报渗透。」布伦妮每次都这样告诉自己。
于是，在若干次「情报渗透」之后，布伦妮终于琢磨出了一套真正可行的策略——将情报收集伪装成游戏，将「拷问」变成「烤问」。
规则很简单。布伦妮问问题，可莉每回答一个就能得到一块现烤的点心作为奖励。问题由浅入深、层层推进，在对象毫无戒备的状态下逐步接近核心情报区。
「可莉，最近妈妈有给你写信吗？」
「有哦。妈妈经常给我写信，不写信的时候我们用嘟嘟通讯仪聊天。布伦妮姐姐，我要巧克力味的饼干。」
「妈妈的罪行…呃…所作所为可莉知道吗？」
「醉行？不可以哦，妈妈说大人的饮料小孩子是不可以喝的。布伦妮姐姐也不可以喝哦，我们喝牛奶吧！」
「妈妈有据点吗？呃，或者说，秘密基地？」
「秘密基地！可莉知道哦。」
「在哪？！」
「——心里！因为妈妈说，每个人最安全的秘密基地就在自己的心里！」
历时一个下午的「烤问」，布伦妮收获了可莉画的七张「情报图」：其中五张的主题是「蹦蹦炸弹的用法」，一张是嘟嘟可的全家福，最后一张是可莉坚持要画的「布伦妮姐姐和可莉一起炸鱼」。另外，布伦妮无意中学习了蹦蹦炸弹的安全引爆距离，也正是在这时，她确认了可莉对于「安全」一词的理解存在重大偏差。
傍晚时分，阿贝多来接可莉，温柔地问她今天玩得开不开心。
可莉拍了拍自己圆滚滚的肚子，露出了一个甜甜的笑容：
「布伦妮姐姐做的饼干太好吃啦！可莉今天不用吃晚饭了！」
与此同时，另一边，布伦妮正严肃整理「首轮烤问行动执行报告」。
「有效情报：无。消耗物资：面粉一袋、牛奶五瓶、巧克力和糖大量。备注：目标疑似具备天生的反审讯天赋，暂无破解手段。」
她顿了顿，又在最下面补了一行很小的字：
「…那张『一起炸鱼』其实还行。已收入档案夹。」

### 「诱巫饵铃」与「猎魔誓锤」

德斯卡戴遭遇「绑架」后，布伦妮陷入了前所未有的焦虑。
她把自己关在房间里，像个即将上战场的将士，开始复盘与艾莉丝交锋的每一个细节：只要辩论进程来到艾莉丝词穷之际，那个女人就会突然笑眯眯掏出一件「珍藏品」，作为布伦妮战胜魔女的奖励。
当时她只觉得这是哄小孩的把戏，就像大人们惯常做的那样，用颗糖果换你闭嘴。
但现在，一个尖锐的疑问浮上心头：倘若魔女那般神通广大，她随手拿出的这些「小玩意儿」，难道真的只是玩具吗？
于是她把战利品一一摊开，进行了一次严肃的战力盘点。剔除了那些明显的「干扰项」：包装花哨的各类零食，低幼图案的闪光贴纸，无特殊功能的毛绒玩具。最终，两个最具实战潜能的物件进入了她的视线。
其一，一个做工精致的小铃铛。「铃铛响起，魔女就有概率出现」，根据艾莉丝语焉不详的说明，布伦妮推测，这是一个实打实的魔女诱捕器。她并未深究「概率」一词的模糊性，因此自然不会想到，这原本只是很久前用来宣告茶会开始的摇铃，得空的魔女会随心情赴约。如今被布伦妮重新定义后，铃铛确实开始在她的冒险中发挥作用——虽然响起时出现的不一定是魔女，但总能指引她走向一些不可思议的奇遇。
其二，则是一把充满视觉冲击的重锤。在诸多战利品中，它体积最大，分量最沉，抡起来虎虎生风，视觉压迫感十足。她并不知道，这把锤子的前身，是艾莉丝原本打算送给另一位魔女尼可的礼物——一件雕塑工具。只是尼可以「爱好是雕塑不是拆迁」为由，婉拒了这份过于豪迈的心意。于是，这把无处可去的锤子，顺理成章成了艾莉丝清库存时的绝佳选择。
这些来自「大魔王」的馈赠，经过布伦妮理性的再诠释与务实的武器化改造，被郑重赋予了新的名字——
「诱巫饵铃」与「猎魔誓锤」。

### 神之眼

布伦妮曾听说「只要愿望足够强烈，神明便会投下视线」。倘若此言非虚，那只要「想要一枚雷元素神之眼」的执念足够深，理论上就能成功兑现。
至于为什么是「雷」？她用于说服自己的理由非常充分：火过于热烈，水太过温润，岩略显沉重，冰有点孤高，风又偏随性，草稍显活泼…唯有雷，冷酷迅捷，正是用来制裁那位狡猾魔女的不二之选。
事实上不难发现，以上理由都有些牵强。那个决定性的因素，或许仅仅是因为紫色的神之眼，恰好与她精心挑选的暗黑重金属风穿搭最般配。
目标既已明确，这位在自己的成长课题上总是过于超前的猎人，毫无意外地采取了一种超前的手段——提前预习。
她将艾莉丝给的那本《元素力使用手册》反复研读了三遍，有关雷元素的知识已烂熟于心，脑内更是模拟了数套以雷元素为核心的「猎魔连招」。
然而，实战演练却卡了壳。改造后的「猎魔誓锤」实在太重了，即便布伦妮依据杠杆原理加长了锤柄，又试图用巧劲发力，举起来依旧极其吃力，更别提拿来战斗了。
「小说里那些角色怎么就这么轻松能拎起比自己大几倍的武器呢？」
她叹了口气，脑海中突然浮现出童话书中召唤力量的台词。
「笑死，那种东西小孩子才会信吧。」她不屑地撇了撇嘴。
「但话又说回来…这不跟向神明许愿是一个道理吗？试一试好像也不亏。」
「醒醒啊！又不是在过家家！」
「但反正周围也没人…」
一阵激烈的思想斗争后，布伦妮红着脸，咬紧牙关，尝试着投入：
「隐藏黑暗力量的『猎魔誓锤』啊，在我面前显现你真正的力量吧！解除封印——」话音刚落，她闭着眼睛猛地握紧锤柄。神奇的是，原本沉重无比的铁锤，此刻竟轻盈得如同握着一支羽毛笔！
种种迹象证明，愿望兑现了。
「就、就这么简单吗？」
她不可置信地伸出另一只手，感受着元素力在掌心迅速汇聚、凝结。随着光芒逐渐亮起，极度紧张与期待的布伦妮下意识地闭紧了双眼，连呼吸都停滞了一瞬。很快，一枚沉甸甸的神之眼落入手心，她满怀着胜利的喜悦，轻轻地睁开双眼，准备迎接那象征狂暴与冷酷的紫色雷霆辉光——
然而，躺在手心的，却是一枚散发着青色流光的、纯净的风元素神之眼。
布伦妮足足愣了十数秒。
「…什么？！」
布伦妮脑海中飞速闪过自己熟读的雷元素笔记，感到一阵强烈的计划外的恼火。
她忍不住对着天空发出质问：「有谁管管吗？搞错了吧——我预习的是雷元素啊！！！」
经过了短暂的错愕后，猎人的理智占据了上风。无论如何，这是一枚真实不虚的神之眼，是力量的凭证。
学会接受意料之外亦是成长的一环。布伦妮迅速收拾好心情，默默将这枚神之眼擦拭干净。「无所谓，大不了再把风元素的章节重学一遍就是了。」毕竟，强者从不抱怨现状，哪怕只是一阵微风，她也要用它吹散魔女的陷阱。
她小声嘟囔着，嘴角却不经意间扬起了一丝连自己都没察觉的弧度。

text.ru:
О том, когда именно началась вражда Прюн с «ведьмой» и в чём её суть, можно узнать из разрозненных записей в «воззваниях к свержению», расклеенных на многих досках объявлений Мондштадта.
Реакцию жителей Мондштадта на рассказы об «охотнице на ведьм» можно разделить на три типа.
Первый тип - это добрая улыбка и предложение выпить яблочного сидра. Второй тип - понимающее кивание и вопрос о том, где её родители. Третий тип, представленный Кэйей и ему подобными, не только внимательно выслушать план мести, но и дать конкретные советы вне зависимости от того, насколько они выполнимы.
Прюн не устраивал ни один из этих трёх типов реакции.
Но как бы она ни была недовольна, она уже давно к этому привыкла. С её точки зрения, быть непонятой - удел охотника, а то, что её воспринимают как ребёнка, играющего во взрослую жизнь, - это взгляды, с которыми она сталкивается каждый день.
По правде, если говорить только о широте кругозора, Прюн действительно опережала многих сверстников и даже некоторых взрослых. Она говорила логично и точно, иногда невзначай выдавая неожиданные малоизвестные факты, и при этом на её лице появлялось едва заметное самодовольное выражение - это были редкие мгновения, когда проявлялась её детскость.
Но при более близком знакомстве становится ясно, что её познания хоть и обширны, но не глубоки. В конце концов, что действительно важного и глубокого может знать ребёнок? Поэтому её гордость - эрудиция - в критические моменты часто даёт сбой перед лицом реальности. Всякий раз в таких случаях она кусает губы, бросает фразу «я ещё вернусь» и быстро ретируется.
Однако, если кто-то решит, что она всего лишь упрямая малышка, которая важничает, его ждёт разочарование.
Потому что, когда она говорит «вернусь», она действительно возвращается. На следующий день она обязательно снова явится с более вескими аргументами, более толстой тетрадью и ещё более несговорчивым выражением лица.
Это упрямое нежелание признавать поражение - то ли врождённый талант охотника, то ли детское упрямство - возможно, даже она сама не может этого понять. Но одно можно сказать точно: сколько бы попыток ни потребовалось, она сведёт счёты со «злой ведьмой» раз и навсегда.
text.en:
Just exactly when Prune's grudge against the "witch" began, and what it is all about, is a story that remains untold by the many "writs of retribution" that can be found on the noticeboards of Mondstadt.
Ask any Mondstadter about the time they met the so-called "Witch Hunter," and you will likely hear of one of three types of reactions.
Those in the first group would tell you that they offered a kind smile before asking if she wanted a glass of Apple Cider. Those of the second persuasion would merely nod vaguely at her words, then ask where her family was. Finally, there are those like Kaeya, the third kind, who would not only listen to her plans of revenge, but also offer their own suggestions, practicable or otherwise.
To Prune, none of these three kinds of treatment is satisfactory.
Nevertheless, she had long since grown used to these responses. She has come to see that it is the fate of the hunter to be utterly misunderstood — and being treated like a child playing house is something she has to face every day.
The fact is, Prune does stand head and shoulders above her peers, and even some adults, in the sheer breadth of her knowledge. Her speech is clear, careful, and organized, and sometimes punctuated by a random factoid thrown in for good measure. The only real indication that she is still just a child is the look of quiet pride that unfailingly accompanies those little-known facts.
But once you get to know her better, you realize that the breadth of her knowledge and understanding is not exactly matched by a corresponding depth — after all, how much of the profound and important can a child really know? Consequently, the "facts" in her arsenal that she is so proud of are often the very ones that lead to her undoing during a "debate." And every time this happens, she bites her lower lip and retreats expeditiously, though not before muttering, "I'll be back."
But it would be a mistake to dismiss her as simply a little girl trying to act tough because of this.
When she says she will be back, return she will. In situations like this, she invariably shows up again the following day, armed with even more notes, more arguments, and an even more mulish look on her face.
Is this stubbornness of hers the grit required of a true hunter or simply childish petulance? This is a question that perhaps even she is unable to answer. One thing, however, she knows for certain: she will settle her score with that "wicked witch" once and for all, no matter how many tries it takes.
text.zh:
布伦妮与魔女之间到底有着怎样苦大仇深的过节，在蒙德许多告示板上的「讨伐檄文」留有些许只言片语的记载。
关于「魔女猎人」的故事，蒙德市民们的反应大致可以分为三类。
第一类是善意的微笑，然后问她要不要来一杯苹果酿。第二类是似懂非懂地点点头，然后问她家里的大人在哪里。第三类是以凯亚为代表的行动派，不但认真聆听她的复仇计划，还会予以具体的建议，无论是否具备可操作性。
布伦妮对以上三种反应都不太满意。
但不满意归不满意，她也已经习惯了。在她看来，不被理解是猎人的宿命，而被当成玩过家家的小孩，更是她每天都要面对的目光。
事实上，单论见识的广度，布伦妮确实领先于许多同龄人乃至部分成年人。她说话条理清晰、措辞严谨，偶尔还会不经意地抛出令人意想不到的冷知识，并因此露出一个藏不住的得意小表情——那是她少数不加掩饰的孩子气瞬间。
但相处久了就会发现，她的知识版图虽然辽阔却并不深厚。毕竟，一个小孩能知道什么真正重要的大事呢？所以她引以为傲的见识，往往在关键时刻被现实反将一军。每当这种时候，她会咬着嘴唇留下一句「我一定会回来的」，头也不回地撤退。
不过，如果有人因此觉得她只是个逞强的小鬼头，那恐怕要失算了。
因为她说「会回来」，就真的会回来。第二天，她一定带着更充分的论据、更厚的笔记和一张更加不服气的脸重新站到你面前。
这种不肯认输的执拗，到底是猎人的天赋还是小孩子的赌气，也许连她自己都分不清楚。但至少有一点可以确定——不管需要折腾多少次，她与那位邪恶魔女之间的账，一笔都不会少算。

text.ru:
Родители Прюн были родом из Мондштадта. В молодости, вдохновлённые романтичными призывами вроде «жизнь - это открытая дорога», они отправились путешествовать по миру и в конце концов осели в Нод-Крае - месте хаоса и возможностей. Эта земля, кишащая торговцами, искателями приключений, мошенниками и контрабандистами, явно не подходила для воспитания ребёнка. Отец надрывался на пристани, мать работала на двух работах сразу, и семья едва сводила концы с концами. Их любовь к Прюн могла проявляться лишь в том, чтобы она была сыта и одета, а время, проведённое вместе, было такой же роскошью, как и искренность для бродяги.
Однажды, в один из редких дней, когда мать вернулась домой до ужина, Прюн выбежала ей навстречу и, ухватившись за край её одежды, с восторгом хотела рассказать, что в городе какой-то торговец продаёт очень красивые игрушечные мельницы из Мондштадта.
Но когда она взяла маму за грубые, натруженные руки и вспомнила о спрятанной отцом в ящике тетради, исписанной цифрами и буквами, где были записаны все их долги, ей пришлось проглотить свои слова.
«Эта штука только и умеет, что крутиться. Совершенно бесполезная вещь, - говорила она себе. - К тому же она мне и не нравится. Нет, совсем не нравится».
Она повторяла себе такие слова снова и снова.
Проходя мимо лавки со сладостями, она тихо утешала себя: «Я давно этого не ем. Сладкое вообще вредно для зубов».
А когда соседские дети хвастались новой блестящей игрушкой, она презрительно фыркала и заявляла, что это «совсем по-детски».
День за днём, уговаривая себя так, маленькая Прюн незаметно возвела вокруг себя высокую стену под названием «взрослость и благоразумие».
Она научилась сама разогревать еду, сама чинить одежду и даже сверять цифры в бухгалтерских книгах. А однажды она обнаружила, что работодатель недоплатил матери за три дня работы. Возможность помочь такими «полезными» делами наполняла её невероятной гордостью.
Каждый вечер, когда мать и отец, совершенно уставшие, возвращались домой, Прюн уже давно была умыта и лежала в кровати, притворяясь спящей. Ведь она знала: если они узнают, что она ещё не спит, что боится темноты, что нуждается в их присутствии, они будут винить себя, расстроятся, и на их и без того тяжкую ношу ляжет ещё один груз вины.
«Я уже не ребёнок». Эта фраза стала её любимой присказкой, а также её доспехами.
text.en:
Prune's parents were Mondstadt natives who, seduced by pithy aphorisms like "Life is an open road," ended up traveling the world. They eventually settled in Nod-Krai, a land of chaos and opportunity. This land, teeming with merchants, adventurers, cheats, smugglers, etc., etc., was clearly no place to start a family and bring up a child. Her dad toiled at the docks while her mom worked two jobs at once. Money was tight, and they only made enough to keep Prune fed and warm. Anything else, like spending time with her, was a luxury they could not afford.
Once, on one of those rare occasions that her mother could make it home before dinner, Prune rushed out to meet her, tugging at her clothes in excitement to tell her about a toy seller in town who was selling these really pretty pinwheels from Mondstadt.
But as she held her mother's hands, made rough by hard work, she recalled the ledger hidden in her father's drawer, a book of numbers and letters detailing all they owed, and decided to bite her tongue instead.
"Those things do nothing but spin," she told herself. "Not exactly the most useful things to have around. Besides, I don't even like them. No, I don't like them at all."
She would tell herself things like this all the time.
Whenever she walked by a candy seller, she would tell herself softly, "I stopped eating these long ago. Sugar is bad for the teeth anyway."
And whenever the kids next door showed off a shiny new toy, she would sniff at them, proclaiming their behavior "totally childish."
Over time, without realizing it, young Prune had built a persona of maturity and poise around herself, a high wall of self-protection that would allow no childish longing to slip through.
She learned to heat her own lunches, mend her clothes, and even how to check the household accounts. Once, she absolutely beamed when she discovered that her mother had been shortchanged three days' pay, as it meant she was actually contributing in a "useful" way.
In the evenings, by the time her parents dragged their weary bodies home, Prune was already all washed up and lying in bed. Though she wasn't actually asleep, she knew her father and mother would be heartbroken to learn she was still afraid of the dark and needed them by her side, and she did not want to weigh down their existing burdens with an added layer of guilt.
"I'm no longer a kid," she would tell herself, and this became not just her pet phrase but her very own suit of armor.
text.zh:
布伦妮的父母是蒙德人，年轻时，他们被「人生是旷野」这类浪漫宣言打动，背着行囊环游世界，最终在挪德卡莱这个充满机遇与混乱的地方定居下来。这里鱼龙混杂，商人、冒险家、骗子、走私贩交织成一张复杂的人际网络，显然不是什么适合养育孩子的地方。爸爸在码头找了个辛苦的差事，妈妈同时打两份工，一家人日子过得紧巴巴。他们对布伦妮的爱只能尽量让她吃饱穿暖，至于陪伴，那就像浪子的真心一样奢侈。
有一次，妈妈难得在晚饭前回家，布伦妮兴奋地跑到门外迎接，攥着妈妈的衣角，迫不及待地想告诉她，镇上有个商人在卖蒙德的风车玩具，特别漂亮。
但当她牵着妈妈粗糙的双手，又想起爸爸藏在抽屉那本密密麻麻记录着欠款的账簿，只好把话咽回肚子里。
「那种只会转圈圈的东西，一点都不实用。」她在心里对自己说「而且我也不喜欢，真的不喜欢。」
这样的话，她已经对自己说过许多次了。
路过糖果摊时，她会小声安慰自己「甜食对牙齿不好，我早就不吃这些了。」
看到隔壁小孩抱着新玩具炫耀，她会扬起下巴说「太幼稚了吧。」
日复一日的自我说服，让小小的布伦妮在不知不觉中，给自己竖起了一道名为「成熟懂事」的高墙。
她学会了自己热饭菜，自己缝补衣服，甚至能帮忙核对账簿上的数字，有一次她还发现雇主少算了妈妈三天的工钱，能帮上这些「有用」的忙，可把自己得意坏了。
每天夜里，当爸爸妈妈拖着疲惫的身躯回到家时，布伦妮早就已经洗漱完毕躺在床上，假装睡着了。因为她知道，如果让他们发现自己还醒着、还怕黑、还需要陪伴，他们会自责，会难过，会在本就沉重的负担上再重重压上一层愧疚。
「我已经不是小孩了。」这句话成了她的口头禅，也成了她的盔甲。

text.ru:
К счастью, в мире есть немало увлечений, которые не требуют больших затрат, и чтение - одно из самых выгодных среди них.
Прюн каждый день приходила в «Книги Мимира» и тихо сидела у стеллажей. Хозяин, казалось, не обращал на неё внимания. Он был слишком занят, пытаясь продать инвестиционные купоны состоятельным клиентам. Так самые разные страницы - новые и пожелтевшие - стали для неё первым окном в мир.
У неё не было никакой системы в выборе книг - только обложка и название. Поначалу это были красочные книжки со сказками, но вскоре её перестали устраивать предсказуемые счастливые концы историй. Она начала поглощать всё, до чего могла дотянуться и на чём были буквы. Но, в отличие от библиотек Мондштадта и Сумеру, Нод-Край изобиловал совсем другим: пожелтевшими газетами с выдуманными новостями, вводящими в заблуждение путеводителями, пиратскими переводами романов и увесистыми историческими трудами, которые явно не предназначались для детей.
По мере того как объём прочитанного стремительно рос, Прюн постепенно превратилась в «кладезь информации», обладающий множеством малополезных знаний, и испытывала от этого самодовольное удовольствие. Эти разрозненные и явно не подходившие по возрасту знания слишком рано сложились в её сознании в сложную и довольно мрачную картину мира.
Понимала ли она прочитанное? В большинстве случаев лишь поверхностно, а иногда и вовсе не понимала. Но она с неутомимым энтузиазмом вбирала сложные термины и незнакомые концепции в свою голову, словно собирая блестящие ракушки на пляже.
Соседские дети несколько раз звали её играть, но она либо была погружена в свои мысли и не слышала, либо, присоединившись, инстинктивно начинала анализировать правила игры, используя выученную книжную терминологию.
«Подождите!» - Прюн подняла руку. - «Мне нужно уточнить базовую логику игры».
«У пиратов было явное топографическое преимущество и достаточное временное окно! Их не должны были так быстро разгромить, если только Банда Раковины не использовала чётко не определённую внезапную атаку, что привело к дисбалансу боевой системы!»
Товарищи по играм переглядывались и постепенно перестали приходить к ней.
Прюн не могла не заметить этого отчуждения, но она лишь презрительно фыркнула, показывая, что ей всё равно. В мире столько глубоких загадок, сложных принципов и необъяснимых явлений, ждущих своего часа, - вот во что действительно стоит вкладывать время и силы.
Она и они уже идут разными путями.
text.en:
Fortunately, there are still several pastimes in this world that do not cost very much, and reading is one of them.
Prune visited Mimisbrunnr Books every day to browse the shelves in silence. The owner didn't mind her being there, for he was far too busy pushing investment coupons on his wealthier customers to care about a little girl. So, left to her own devices, she made those myriad pages her very first window into the world.
She had no real system for picking what to read next; all that mattered was whether the cover or the title caught her eye. She started with the fairy tales with the colorful book jackets, but soon grew tired of the easy tropes and predictable happily-ever-afters. From there, she began to devour anything with text she could lay her hands on. But Nod-Krai wasn't like the libraries of Mondstadt or Sumeru. Instead of grand, curated collections, she found mostly yellowed newspapers or papers with "news" of things that never happened, terribly misleading travel guides, and pirated, poorly translated novels... and plodding historical tomes containing details no child her age was meant to see.
As her reading habit spiraled, Prune gradually became a hoarder of obscure knowledge, a fact she delightedly took as proof of her intellectual superiority. This haphazard collection of out-there facts in her young mind began to frame her understanding of the world in a manner that was complex, yet rather fuzzy, at the same time.
Did she really understand what she was reading? Most of the time, the answer was not really. But she was more than happy to soak up those difficult terms and alien concepts, like someone picking up every shiny seashell in sight on a beach and putting them all into a bag.
Even though she was invited to play with the neighborhood kids a few times, on those occasions, she either failed to hear them because she was lost in her own thoughts, or she spent the time obsessively studying the rules of the game using the vocabulary she had picked up from her books.
"Hold on!" Prune would interject as she raised her hand, "I must check the underlying logic of the game first."
"The Pirates had a real topographical advantage and a sufficient time buffer! They shouldn't have been defeated so quickly, unless the Conch Gang used an undefined Surprise Attack that caused the power-scaling system to fail!"
Slowly, over time, the other kids stopped knocking at her door.
Prune wasn't blind to the other kids drifting away, though she gave a little scoff to show she no longer cared. Besides, there were so many deep questions, complicated principles, and unexplained phenomena in the world waiting for her to apply her intellect and energy to.
She was already on a different path from the others.
text.zh:
好在，世界上还有不少不怎么花钱的爱好，读书就是其中比较划算的一项。
「秘谜书屋」的店主似乎只专注向有购买力的目标客户兜售投资券，并不在意每天前来蹲在书架前安静翻书的布伦妮，于是各种崭新或泛黄的书页成了她最初认知世界的窗口。
她阅读的范围毫无体系，全凭封面和书名是否吸引。起初是色彩斑斓的童话绘本，但她很快便不满足于王子公主的幸福结局。她开始啃噬一切能接触到的、带字的东西。但不像蒙德和须弥的图书馆，挪德卡莱充斥着更多的是：过期或铺满假新闻的报纸、误导性的旅行指南、错版盗版或者翻译有误的小说、若干少儿不宜的沉重历史读物。
随着阅读量的激增，布伦妮渐渐成为了一名掌握诸多冷门知识的「情报强者」，并对这种智力上的优越感产生了一种洋洋自得的愉悦。这些零碎而超前的知识，过早地在她脑海中拼凑出一个复杂且略带灰色的认知框架。
她读得懂吗？大多数时候，只是一知半解，甚至完全不懂。但她乐此不疲地把那些艰深的术语、陌生的概念像收集闪亮的贝壳一样装进脑子里。
邻里孩子们玩耍时邀请过她几次，但她要么沉浸在自己的思绪中没听见，要么加入后，下意识地用她学来的词汇去分析游戏规则。
「等一下！」布伦妮举起手，「我需要确认一下游戏的底层逻辑。」
「『海盗』拥有足够的地形优势和时间缓冲窗口，不应该这么快被攻破，除非『海螺帮』使用了未被明确定义的『奇袭』，导致战力系统失衡！」
玩伴们面面相觑，渐渐便不再来找她。
布伦妮并非没有察觉这种疏离，但她冷哼一声，表示已经无所谓了。世界上有那么多深奥的谜题、复杂的原理、等待被理解的现象，那些才是真正值得投入时间的东西。
她和他们，已经走在不同的路上了。

text.ru:
Это был день, полный смутных мыслей и беспокойства.
Хотя взрослые предупреждали, что «детям не стоит читать такие книги», Прюн всё же с трудом осилила первый том «Гимнов крайнего севера». Закрыв книгу, она обнаружила, что забыла начало, забыла конец, да и середину толком не помнила. Эти туманные стихи оказались слишком сложными для нынешней Прюн. Усталость от интеллектуальных поисков и одиночество, которое бывает, когда не с кем поделиться мыслями, волнами разливались в её душе.
И поэтому, как обычно, она начала изливать душу пустоте рядом с собой. В её воображении всегда был слушатель - возможно, та бездомная собачка, которую она не могла приютить, или какой-нибудь глубокомысленный мудрец из книги. Она сама не знала, к кому именно обращается, день за днём утоляя свою неутолимую жажду поделиться мыслями. Но на этот раз воздух слегка дрогнул, и воображаемый друг отчётливо материализовался перед её глазами.
«Это ты... Ты и есть...»
Прюн взволнованно хотела выкрикнуть его имя, но только сейчас поняла, что всё это время в своих мыслях называла его просто «ты».
Нужно было срочно придумать ему имя. Прежде всего - исключить всякие «Пушистик», «Шарик» или «Малыш». Очевидно, это не обычный ласковый пёсик: чуть нахмуренные брови выдавали необыкновенную мудрость, глубокие глаза скрывали его внутренний мир, а самое главное - его сине-фиолетовая, с проблесками лилового шерсть была такой таинственной и серьёзно выглядящей, что прекрасно соответствовала нынешним эстетическим предпочтениям Прюн.
«Декарт», - наконец выдохнула она. Это имя, пропитанное духом философского поиска и любопытства, идеально подходило её спутнику. И заодно демонстрировало её собственную выдающуюся эрудицию.
Появление Декарта не только значительно обогатило жизнь Прюн, но и сделало её ещё более... эксцентричной.
Однажды днём после дождя Прюн присела у обочины, наблюдая за отражением в луже.
«Смотри, Декарт, мир в этой луже перевёрнут. Вот и вопрос: как нам доказать, что мы сами не живём в какой-то "большой луже"?»
Несколько прохожих замедлили шаг и с недоумением уставились на маленькую девочку, которая разговаривала сама с собой, сидя у лужи.
«Возможно, - продолжила Прюн, - то, что мы считаем «реальностью», всего лишь «отражение» некоего существа из более высокого измерения. Ты согласен, Декарт?»
Она повернулась к пустому месту рядом с собой и серьёзно кивнула:
«Да, я тоже так думаю. Поэтому в своей основе всякое познание относительно, а абсолютной истины не существует».
Добрая пожилая женщина подошла и с участием спросила: «Девочка, ты... в порядке? Может, помочь найти твоих родных?»
Прюн подняла голову и искренне спросила: «Бабушка, вы верите, что этот мир настоящий?»
«...»
«Ладно, многие люди до старости ни разу не задумываются над этим вопросом», - Прюн встала. - «Пошли, Декарт. Может быть, жизнь в иллюзии - это и есть обычная жизнь большинства взрослых».
Старушка проводила взглядом странную девочку и невольно сложила руки в молитвенном жесте на груди: «Пусть луна хранит тебя, дитя...»
Однако улочки и переулки Нашгорода были узкими и запутанными, и милосердный лунный свет не мог проникнуть во все тёмные закоулки.
А в тех холодных, одиноких углах, куда свет не доходил, единственным «оберегом» Прюн был Декарт - безмолвный, но вечно преданный спутник.
text.en:
It was an afternoon filled with restlessness and tangled thoughts.
Through sheer perseverance, Prune finally managed to finish the first volume of the "Hymns of the Far North" even though an adult had warned her it might be unsuitable for a child her age. Yet, by the time she snapped the book shut, she could hardly remember what she had actually read. All that remained was the sheer weight of those verses, a sense of mental exhaustion, and the loneliness of having no one to talk to about any of it.
And so, in her usual fashion, she began to expound to the empty air about what she had just read. In her mind's eye, she saw a little puppy listening, a creature that material circumstances made impossible for one to actually adopt. Or maybe she was speaking to one of the sages from the book who never said a word in return. She wasn't quite sure whom she was talking to, only that she needed to talk. But this time, the air began to stir, and the friend so alive in her imagination began to take shape before her.
"It's you... You are..."
Oh, how Prune wished to call it by its name! But it was at this juncture that she realized that she had been addressing it by "you" all this time.
It was imperative that the creature be given a name of its own then and there. The common names like "Floofy," "Barky," and "Tiny" were out, since it was clear to her that this was no ordinary fawning puppy. It had a keen intelligence visible in its slightly raised brows and a peculiar depth in the dark pools of its eyes. Its indigo and violet-dappled coat had a sense of mystery and steadiness that Prune found deeply pleasing.
"Descartes," she finally breathed. It was a perfect name, full of the spirit of intellectual inquiry and curiosity, and entirely worthy of a companion of hers. It was a name that also spoke to her own formidable erudition.
Descartes' arrival added quite the burst of color to Prune's daily life, even as it turned her into even more of a... lone ranger.
One afternoon, after the rains had come and gone. Prune was crouching by the street, studying a puddle intently.
"Look, Descartes. Look at how the world is upside down in the puddle. That raises the question: how do we know that we aren't just living in a bigger puddle?"
Several passersby slowed down as they passed, staring at the girl who appeared to be talking to herself.
"Maybe," Prune continued, "what we know as 'real' is just another 'reflection' in a higher dimension. Don't you agree, Descartes?"
She turned towards the air around her and nodded gravely.
"Yes, that's what I think too. So fundamentally, all knowledge is relative, and absolute truth does not exist."
Finally, a kind old lady walked over to her and asked with a touch of concern, "Hey, little girl. Are you... alright? Do you need me to call your family?"
Prune looked up and replied earnestly, "Hey granny. Do you believe that the world is real?"
"..."
"Never mind. That's not a question everyone asks even once in their lifetime anyway," Prune concluded. "Let's go, Descartes. Maybe living in an illusion is just what most adults do."
As she watched the odd little girl leave, the old lady made a sign over her chest and murmured, "May the moon watch over you, my child..."
However, there are corners in Nasha Town's narrow and winding streets and alleys where the light of the moon's blessing simply cannot reach.
And in those cold, solitary corners, the only "blessing" Prune had was Descartes, her silent but ever-faithful companion.
text.zh:
那是一个思绪纷扰的下午。
尽管有大人告诫过「小孩子不要看这些书」，但布伦妮依旧艰难地啃完了《终北祷歌集》的第一卷。合上书，前面忘了、后面忘了、中间也记不太清了，但那些晦涩的诗句，对现在的布伦妮而言还是太过沉重，智力探索的疲惫与无人分享的孤独感，如潮水般弥漫开来。
于是，她像往常一样，对着身旁的空气开启了单方面的长篇大论。在她的想象中，一直有一个倾听者——或许是那只受限于现实条件没法收养的流浪小狗，或许是书中某个深沉寡言的智者。她不知道自己究竟在对「谁」说话，只是日复一日地将无处安放的分享欲倾注其中。可是这一次，空气轻轻晃动，幻想中的朋友清晰地浮现在眼前。
「是你吧…你就是…」
布伦妮激动地想要呼喊它的名字，却才发现，自己在脑海中一直以「你」来称呼它。
当务之急是取一个名字。首先要排除「毛球」、「大嗓门」或「小不点」之类的宠物名。显然，它不是那种爱撒娇的寻常小狗：微微皱起的眉头彰显别样的睿智，眼眸深邃叫人看不透它的内心，最令人叫绝的是它那蓝紫交杂的毛色，如此神秘又沉稳，非常符合布伦妮当前的审美。
「德斯卡戴」，她选择了这个充满哲学思辨气息的名字，既确立了伙伴的格调，又顺便展现了自己卓越的学识。
德斯卡戴的到来，不仅极大地丰富了布伦妮的生活，也让她显得更…特立独行。
一个雨后的下午，布伦妮蹲在街边观察水坑里的倒影。
「你看，德斯卡戴，这个水坑里的世界和真实的世界是完全相反的。那么问题来了，我们怎么证明自己不是活在某个『更大的水坑』里呢？」
路过的行人放慢脚步，用奇怪的眼神看着这个对着水坑自言自语的小女孩。
「也许，」布伦妮继续说，「我们所认为的『真实』，只是某种更高维度存在的『倒影』。你说对不对，德斯卡戴？」
她转头看向身边的空气，严肃地点了点头。
「嗯，我也这么想。所以本质上，所有的认知都是相对的，绝对真理根本不存在。」
一位好心的老婆婆走过来，关切地问：「小妹妹，你…没事吧？要不要帮你找找家人？」
布伦妮抬起头，眼神真诚地问：「这位婆婆，您相信这个世界是真实的吗？」
「…」
「算了，很多人到老都没思考过这个问题。」布伦妮站起来，「走吧，德斯卡戴，也许活在虚影中就是许多大人的日常。」
老婆婆目送着这个古怪的小女孩离开，忍不住在胸口比了个祈祷的手势：「愿月光庇佑你，孩子…」
然而，那夏镇的街巷逼仄错落，悲悯的月光终有照不透的幽暗角落。
在这冰冷的现实缝隙里，真正如影随形「庇佑」着布伦妮的，其实只有德斯卡戴——它无声却恒久的陪伴，填满了小女孩所有的独处时光。

text.ru:
Скучающая ведьма, которой нужно было чем-то себя занять, наконец заметила девочку, которая сидела на обочине, уткнувшись в огромную книгу.
«Почему бы тебе не пойти поиграть с теми детьми?»
«Не люблю "играть"», - Прюн даже не подняла головы, продолжая листать книгу.
«А что это ты читаешь?» - заглянула через плечо Алиса, и её глаза загорелись, словно она обнаружила сокровище.
«Что-то вроде туристического путеводителя. Но это пиратская копия, напечатаны только отрывки, автор неизвестен...»
«И как тебе?»
«Ничего особенного. Чувствуется, что автор ещё тот эгоцентрик, а между строк сквозит немало дурного вкуса».
«О-ой?»
С того дня Алиса то и дело «случайно» оказывалась рядом с Прюн, подкидывая ей самые неожиданные темы для обсуждения: «Если добавить сахар в Гидро слайма, получится сладкий напиток?», «Если я победила себя - это я выиграла или проиграла?»...
Каждый раз, когда Алиса бросала один из этих бессмысленных вызовов, Прюн воспринимала его как реальную угрозу и пыталась опровергнуть эти «ложные утверждения» строгими логическими цепочками - неуклюже и очень мило. Алиса же, подперев щёку рукой, с улыбкой наблюдала за пытливой девочкой, словно за интересным представлением.
Алиса, весьма проницательная, заметила, что воображаемый друг рядом с Прюн становился всё бледнее, словно утренняя роса, готовая вот-вот испариться под лучами солнца.
Поначалу Прюн не придавала значения тому, что Алиса называла себя «ведьмой». В Нод-Крае даже простой дядька в очереди за «Лунным угощением» мог представиться великим вором, ушедшим на покой. Так что появление очередной странной тётки в ажурной шляпе, с вычурным вкусом и ребяческими речами вполне вписывалось в местный колорит.
Она и представить не могла, что однажды эта инфантильная особа станет её заклятым врагом, словно по велению судьбы...
«Доказать соотношение сторон треугольника без математики - для тебя уже нетрудно? Впечатляет».
«Тётя, это максимум задача для двенадцатилетних».
«Тогда задам тебе задачку для четырнадцатилетних, давай посоревнуемся!»
«Ты можешь справляться только с задачами такого уровня? Мне пора домой ужинать. Пошли, Декарт».
К сожалению, к этому моменту Декарт уже стал почти прозрачным, настолько слабым, что вот-вот должен был исчезнуть.
Увидев это, Алиса тут же изобразила преувеличенно злодейское выражение лица. «Хм-хм! - зловеще рассмеялась она. - На самом деле я - злая великая ведьма! И сейчас я забираю твоего самого дорогого друга!»
«Ч-что?!»
«Его!» - Алиса щёлкнула пальцами, и Прюн перестала видеть Декарта.
Умная головка Прюн в этот момент дала редкий сбой.
«Ты что, ненормальная?! - она вцепилась в подол платья Алисы. - Что ты сделала с Декартом?! Верни его мне немедленно!»
«Уже поздно! С этого момента ты официально признана мной, величайшей ведьмой, как "охотница на ведьм"! Хочешь спасти своего воображаемого друга - сначала победи меня!»
«Это поведение настоящей злодейки! Немедленно верни мне Декарта!»
«Ха-ха, шучу, - Алиса рассмеялась ещё беззаботнее. - Чтобы спасти своего воображаемого друга, тебе придётся победить всех ведьм на свете! Да-да, каждая получит по заслугам».
«Отправляйся в Мондштадт и найди Кли. Эта самая милая девочка на свете, возможно, даст тебе подсказку».
Сказав это, Алиса исчезла в облаке дыма.
«Али-и-иса! Ты бесстыжая злодейка!»
Так началось долгое и трудное путешествие бедной Прюн по спасению друга.
text.en:
The bored witch, who had been looking for some kind of distraction, finally found a target in the girl buried deep in a giant book by the side of the road.
"Why aren't you playing with those kids over there?"
"I don't like to play." Prune, who was busy reading, didn't even look up.
"What are we looking at?" Alice moved her head to get a better look, and her eyes lit up as if she'd just discovered some kind of treasure.
"A travel guide of some sort. But it's missing some parts because it's a pirated copy. I'm not sure who the writer is..."
"How do you find it?"
"It's okay, I guess. But I can't help feeling that the writer is just incredibly self-absorbed. I also caught some troubling tendencies between the lines."
"Oh?"
From that day on, Alice would "run into" Prune every now and then, always hitting her with the most random of questions like "Does adding sugar to a Hydro Slime make it a sweet drink?" or "If you conquer yourself, do you actually win or lose?"
And each time Alice dropped one of these nonsensical provocations, Prune would rise to the challenge as if the enemy were literally at the gates, and scramble to dismantle these "false propositions" with her impeccable logic in a way that was both clumsy and adorable. Meanwhile, Alice would watch her exertions with chin in hand and a smile on her face, like she was taking in some sort of performance.
Through all of this, the astute Alice also noticed Prune's imaginary friend slowly fading, like a drop of water evaporating in the sunlight.
At first, Prune did not really care for the fact that Alice was a mage. After all, in Nod-Krai, it is not unusual to encounter individuals claiming to be one extraordinary character or another. Even the average guy queuing up for a Feast of the Moon might claim he's a retired master thief. A strange, flamboyant woman in an oversized hat who engaged in mischievous banter? She would totally fit in with the rest of the local personalities.
Little did she know that one day, this cheeky woman would turn into her nemesis...
"Did you just use a non-arithmetic method to prove the relationship between the sides of a triangle? Impressive."
"Excuse me, but that's a problem even a 12-year-old can solve."
"Well, how about we try another problem for 14-year-olds then? Come on, let's see who can solve it first!"
"Don't you have anything better to do than hang out with someone my age? I have to go home for dinner. Let's go, Descartes."
But unfortunately, Descartes had almost faded into nothingness by this point.
Seeing this, Alice immediately threw herself into the role of a stage villain. "Heh heh heh..." she cackled. "I am actually a wicked, wicked witch! And now, I'm snatching away your most precious friend!"
"W—What!?"
"I'm talking about... HIM!" Then, Alice snapped her fingers, and Descartes vanished from Prune's sight.
Quick-witted as Prune usually is, she experienced a rare system crash at this moment.
"What is wrong with you!?" she cried, clutching desperately at Alice. "What did you do to Descartes??? I want my Descartes back!!!"
"Oops, too late~ From now on, you're officially the 'Witch Hunter' appointed by the Great Mage herself! Want to save your imaginary friend? You'll have to beat me first!"
"You call yourself a mage??? That's straight-up witch behavior! Give me back my Descartes now!!!"
"Ha! Just kidding!" Alice was really hitting her stride with that wicked laugh now. "Want your imaginary friend back? Then you'll have to best every mage on the planet! That's right, you'll have to beat them all up, one by one!"
"Go to Mondstadt and find Klee. Who knows, you might just get a clue or two from the world's most adorable little kid."
Then, Alice disappeared with a bang and a poof.
"ALICE!!! YOU BIG, FAT, BADDIE!!!"
And so, poor Prune's long, arduous campaign to save her friend began.
text.zh:
百无聊赖的魔女为了打发时间盯上了路边正在抱着一本大书研读的小女孩。
「怎么不去跟那边的小孩玩？」
「不喜欢『玩』。」布伦妮甚至没有抬起头，继续翻书。
「在看什么呢？」艾莉丝探头一看，眼睛明显亮了起来，像是发现了什么宝藏。
「游览指南之类的东西。但因为是盗版，只印了些段落，作者不详…」
「你觉得写得怎么样？」
「还行吧。感觉作者是那种极度以自我为中心的人，字里行间还透露出不少恶趣味。」
「哦呀？」
从那天起，艾莉丝隔三岔五就会「碰巧」出现在布伦妮身边，抛出五花八门的话题，发起「辩论」：「往水史莱姆里加糖会不会得到一杯甜味饮料？」「战胜自我是我赢了还是我输了？」…
艾莉丝沉浸在这种无厘头的挑衅上，而布伦妮则每次都如临大敌，她会试图用严谨的逻辑链条去驳斥这些「伪命题」，过于认真的模样透露着笨拙的可爱。艾莉丝则托着腮，笑眯眯地看着她引经据典，仿佛在欣赏一场有趣的演出。
与此同时，艾莉丝敏锐地注意到，布伦妮身边那位幻想朋友的身影正变得越来越淡，如同阳光下即将蒸发的晨露。
起初，布伦妮对于艾莉丝的「魔女」身份不以为意。在挪德卡莱，连一起排队领「飨月餐」的普通大叔，都可能自称是隐退的神奇大盗。那么，街头多出一个头戴镂空大帽子、审美浮夸且言辞幼稚的奇怪阿姨，也完全符合这里的人类多样性。
她没想到，有一天，这个大龄幼稚鬼竟会成为她命定般的宿敌——
「用数学以外的方法证明三角形边长的关系，这种难题已经难不倒你了吗？了不起。」
「阿姨，这个最多属于十二岁水平的基础题。」
「那么，我再出一个十四岁级别的题，来比比看吧！」
「你是只能在这个年龄段混了吗？我要回家吃饭了。德斯卡戴，我们走。」
可惜这时的德斯卡戴已经几近透明，微弱得快要消失了。
「哼哼。」艾莉丝突然摆出一副极其夸张的反派表情，「我其实是邪恶的大魔女！现在…我要把你最重要的朋友抓走！」
「什…什么？」
「就是『它』啦——」艾莉丝打了个清脆的响指，德斯卡戴的身影在布伦妮眼中彻底消散了。
布伦妮聪明的小脑瓜罕见地宕机了。
「你有病吧！」她死死抓住艾莉丝的衣角，「你对德斯卡戴做了什么！快还给我！」
「来不及了哦~从现在开始，你就是被大魔女亲自认证的『魔女猎人』了！想要救回你的幻想朋友，就必须先打败我！」
「魔女和巫婆是有本质区别的好吧！这根本是巫婆行为！快把德斯卡戴还给我！」
「嗯哼，骗你的。」艾莉丝笑得更加肆意了，「想要救回幻想朋友，要打败世界上所有的魔女才可以哦！对，她们也要挨揍。」
「到蒙德去吧，找到可莉，那位全天下最可爱的小朋友说不定会给你线索哦。」
说完，艾莉丝便消失在爆炸后的烟雾中。
「艾——莉——丝！你这个无耻的大反派！」
就这样，布伦妮漫长的讨伐之旅，在气急败坏的咆哮中正式拉开帷幕。

text.ru:
Едва прибыв в Мондштадт, Прюн немедленно приступила к «охоте на ведьм». Она расклеивала провокационные объявления, пыталась публично уличить астролога Мону (предполагаемую «ведьму Б.»), выслеживала людей, связанных с её целями... Но по мере того как одна операция за другой проваливались, ей пришлось задуматься о более радикальных мерах.
В голову пришла безумная мысль - похитить Кли и заставить Алису объявиться.
План был прост: заманить цель в безлюдное место, подвергнуть допросу и получить нужную информацию о местонахождении ведьмы. Прюн даже набросала в блокноте алгоритм действий.
Конечно же, как охотница на ведьм, имеющая свои принципы, она быстро отвергла этот план.
Не потому, что за спиной Кли стояли Альбедо и весь Ордо Фавониус (хотя это, безусловно, было самым серьёзным практическим препятствием), а потому, что в противостоянии разума и импульса мораль в конце концов одержала верх над ненавистью.
«"Кто сражается с чудовищами, должен остерегаться, чтобы самому не стать чудовищем"... Банально! Что за честь - обижать ребёнка? Я не стану совершать такой бесчестный поступок».
Она зачеркнула «алгоритм действий» большим крестом, перевернула страницу, написала «План Б» и в задумчивости уставилась на пустой лист.
Пока Прюн размышляла над следующей стратегией, Кли бесчисленное количество раз врывалась к ней с угощениями и игрушками.
«Сестрёнка Прюн, сегодня солнышко! Пошли ловить кристальных бабочек!»
«Сестрёнка Прюн, сегодня дождь, пойдём найдём братика Мини-Дурина и попрыгаем по лужам!»
«Прю-ю-ю-юн...»
Так или иначе, Прюн обнаружила, что незаметно для себя стала напарницей Кли по играм. Её «заставили» рисовать граффити на стенах вместе с Кли, слушать, как та с серьёзным видом рассказывает про имя и характер каждого Додоко, и даже кататься вместе с ней по травянистому склону в долине Ветров...
«...Я просто собираю разведывательные данные для задуманного», - каждый раз повторяла себе Прюн.
Наконец, после нескольких таких «разведывательных вылазок» Прюн разработала по-настоящему действенную стратегию: замаскировать сбор информации под игру, превратив «допрос» в «Угощенье за вопрос».
Правила были очень просты: Прюн задаёт вопросы, а за каждый ответ Кли получает свежеиспечённое лакомство. Вопросы начинались с простых и постепенно углублялись, шаг за шагом приближаясь к ключевой информации, пока объект оставался совершенно беспечным.
«Кли, мама недавно писала тебе письма?»
«Да. Мама часто пишет мне письма, а когда не пишет, мы общаемся через Додокоммуникатор. Сестричка Прюн, я хочу шоколадное печенье».
«Знает ли Кли о преступлениях мамы... э-э... о её деяниях?»
«Пьянство? Нельзя, мама сказала, что детям нельзя пить взрослые напитки. Сестрёнке Прюн тоже нельзя, давай лучше выпьем молока!»
«У мамы есть опорный пункт? Эм, или, может, тайное убежище?»
«Тайное убежище! Кли знает, где оно».
«Где?!»
«В сердце! Потому что мама сказала, что самое безопасное тайное убежище каждого человека находится в его собственном сердце!»
После целого дня таких расспросов Прюн получила семь «информационных рисунков» от Кли: пять из них были инструкциями «Как использовать Прыг-скок бомбу», один - семейный портрет Додоко, а последний - «Сестрёнка Прюн и Кли вместе взрывают рыбу», на котором настояла сама Кли. Кроме того, Прюн совершенно случайно узнала безопасное расстояние для применения Прыг-скок бомбы - и именно в этот момент она поняла, что представление Кли о слове «безопасность» серьёзно отличается от общепринятого.
Ближе к вечеру за Кли пришёл Альбедо и нежно спросил, весело ли она провела день.
Кли расплылась в сладкой улыбке, похлопала себя по круглому животу и сказала:
«Печенье сестрёнки Прюн такое вкусное! Кли сегодня может не ужинать!»
Тем временем Прюн совершенно серьёзно составляла «Отчёт о проведении первого раунда операции "Угощенье за вопрос"».
«Полезная информация: нет. Израсходовано: один мешок муки, пять бутылок молока, большое количество шоколада и сахара. Примечание: цель, по-видимому, обладает врождённым талантом к противодействию допросам, средства преодоления пока не найдены».
Она на мгновение задумалась и добавила внизу ещё одну строку мелкими буквами:
«...Тот рисунок, как мы взрываем рыбу, вообще-то неплох. Добавлено в архив».
text.en:
Prune began her "witch-hunting" campaign as soon as she landed in Mondstadt. She put up notices of a rather antagonistic nature everywhere, tried to publicly confront the astrologer Mona (suspected to be the witch known as "B"), and sought out various individuals linked to her targets... But as the setbacks accumulated, she had no choice but to consider even more robust measures.
A rather outrageous idea came to her mind: she'd abduct Klee to force Alice to show herself.
The plan was simple. She would lure her target to somewhere quiet, then perform a thorough interrogation to obtain the intel she needed. Prune even went so far as to map out the operation in her notebook.
But of course, as a "Witch Hunter" with real scruples, she then very quickly ditched the idea.
She came to this decision not because she would also have to contend with Albedo and all of the Knights of Favonius (although it would admittedly be the greatest obstacle), but because in the tug-of-war between reason and impulse, morality eventually triumphed over hate.
"'He who slays the monster invariably becomes one himself,' right? Please, that's such an unoriginal tactic! I would never stoop to doing something so despicable to a child!"
She drew a giant cross over her "operational plan," flipped to a fresh page, and wrote down two words, "Plan B," before staring blankly at her notebook.
The days passed as Prune plotted her next steps, often to the sounds of Klee walking in on her with snacks or a toy in hand.
"Prune, the sun is out today! Let's go catch some Crystalflies!"
"Prune, it's raining today. Let's go jump some puddles with Mini Durin!"
"Prune~~~"
Then, at some point, Prune realized that she had unwittingly become Klee's playmate, and in this role, she was "forced" to doodle on walls with Klee, listen to her introduce the name and personality of every single Dodoco in complete seriousness, and even roll down the grassy slopes of Windrise...
"...I'm just collecting intel for my operation," Prune would say to herself each time.
Finally, after several of these "intelligence-gathering outings," Prune hammered out a truly viable strategy. She was going to obtain the intel she needed by grilling Klee, not by outright interrogation but by disguising the questioning as a game.
The operation would be a simple and straightforward one. Prune would ask the questions, and reward Klee with a freshly baked morsel each time her target provides an answer. She would start with a broad, innocent question, then probe deeper and deeper from there, gradually closing in on the key intel while her target's guard was completely down.
"Klee, have you received any letters from your mom recently?"
"Yeah, yeah. I get letters from her pretty often, and we also talk on the Dodocomm when there aren't any letters. I'd like a chocolate cookie, please."
"So, Klee, are you aware of your mom's atrocities... um... I mean, actions?"
"A toast with cheese...? I don't remember my mom ever making that. That sounds yummy though! It'll be so good with a glass of milk!"
"Is your mom in some kind of strongh— uh, I mean, does she have some kind of secret base?"
"A secret base! I know where it is!"
"Where is it???"
"It's... in the heart! Mom says that the safest secret base for everyone is in their hearts!"
Prune's rewards from an afternoon of "grilling" were seven drawings by Klee, including five on "How to Use a Jumpy Dumpty" and one depicting a Dodoco family. The last, drawn at Klee's insistence, was titled "Prune and Klee Blow Up Fish Together." Additionally, Prune also learned — quite by accident — the exact safe distance to set off a Jumpy Dumpty, though it was also at this point she realized there was serious work to be done if one ever wanted to bring Klee up to speed on the actual definition of the word "safe."
Early evening. Albedo had come to pick up Klee, asking her tenderly if she had a good day.
Klee let out a bright, cheery smile as she patted her bursting-full tummy in total satisfaction.
"Prune's cookies are so yummy! I won't need dinner tonight!"
Meanwhile, Prune was writing up her "After-Action Report: Operation Bake-and-Grill, Round 1" with utter seriousness.
"Usable intel: None. Supplies consumed: 1 bag of flour, 5 bottles of milk, and a substantial amount of chocolate and sugar. NB: Target appears to have an innate talent for counter-interrogation, and there are currently no known methods that can be used to break her."
She paused for a moment before adding another line below in tiny letters. It read:
"That picture of us blowing up fish together isn't too bad, actually. Filed in dossier."
text.zh:
刚到蒙德，布伦妮便立刻展开了她的猎魔行动。张贴火药味十足的告示、试图与疑似「魔女B」的占星术士莫娜当街对质、走访调查魔女的关系网…但随着各项行动屡屡受挫，她不得不开始考虑一些更极端的手段。
一个疯狂的念头在脑海闪过——绑架可莉，逼艾莉丝现身。
计划很简单：将目标引至人少的地方，进行残酷的拷问，获取魔女的核心情报。布伦妮甚至已经在笔记本上画好了行动路线图。
当然了，作为一个有底线的魔女猎人，她很快就否决了这个方案。
不是因为可莉身后有阿贝多和整个西风骑士团做靠山（当然这无疑是最大的实战阻碍），而是在理智与冲动的拉锯中，道德最终战胜了仇恨。
「『屠龙者终成恶龙』么…老套！欺负小孩算什么嘛，我才不干这种无耻的事。」
她在行动路线图上画上一个大大的叉，翻到新的一页，写下了「方案B」三个字，盯着空白的纸面开始发呆。
在布伦妮盘算下一步策略的那些日子里，可莉数不清多少次拿着零食和玩具推门而入。
「布伦妮姐姐，今天出太阳了，我们去抓晶蝶吧！」
「布伦妮姐姐，今天下雨啦，我们去找小杜林哥哥一起踩水坑！」
「布伦妮姐姐——」
一来二去，布伦妮发现自己不知不觉变成了可莉的玩伴。她「被迫」陪可莉在墙面上涂鸦、听可莉一本正经地介绍每一只嘟嘟可的名字和性格、甚至被拉着在风起地的草坡上打滚…
「…我是在做情报渗透。」布伦妮每次都这样告诉自己。
于是，在若干次「情报渗透」之后，布伦妮终于琢磨出了一套真正可行的策略——将情报收集伪装成游戏，将「拷问」变成「烤问」。
规则很简单。布伦妮问问题，可莉每回答一个就能得到一块现烤的点心作为奖励。问题由浅入深、层层推进，在对象毫无戒备的状态下逐步接近核心情报区。
「可莉，最近妈妈有给你写信吗？」
「有哦。妈妈经常给我写信，不写信的时候我们用嘟嘟通讯仪聊天。布伦妮姐姐，我要巧克力味的饼干。」
「妈妈的罪行…呃…所作所为可莉知道吗？」
「醉行？不可以哦，妈妈说大人的饮料小孩子是不可以喝的。布伦妮姐姐也不可以喝哦，我们喝牛奶吧！」
「妈妈有据点吗？呃，或者说，秘密基地？」
「秘密基地！可莉知道哦。」
「在哪？！」
「——心里！因为妈妈说，每个人最安全的秘密基地就在自己的心里！」
历时一个下午的「烤问」，布伦妮收获了可莉画的七张「情报图」：其中五张的主题是「蹦蹦炸弹的用法」，一张是嘟嘟可的全家福，最后一张是可莉坚持要画的「布伦妮姐姐和可莉一起炸鱼」。另外，布伦妮无意中学习了蹦蹦炸弹的安全引爆距离，也正是在这时，她确认了可莉对于「安全」一词的理解存在重大偏差。
傍晚时分，阿贝多来接可莉，温柔地问她今天玩得开不开心。
可莉拍了拍自己圆滚滚的肚子，露出了一个甜甜的笑容：
「布伦妮姐姐做的饼干太好吃啦！可莉今天不用吃晚饭了！」
与此同时，另一边，布伦妮正严肃整理「首轮烤问行动执行报告」。
「有效情报：无。消耗物资：面粉一袋、牛奶五瓶、巧克力和糖大量。备注：目标疑似具备天生的反审讯天赋，暂无破解手段。」
她顿了顿，又在最下面补了一行很小的字：
「…那张『一起炸鱼』其实还行。已收入档案夹。」

title.ru: Колокольчик-приманка и Клятвенный молот охотницы на ведьм

text.ru:
